tahino-pie-argiro.name: Tahino Pie

ingredients.item: 1 cup of flour inflated by itself
1 cup of flour inflated by itself

instructions: Crush the yeast in a bowl, sprinkle with some sugar, add 2-3 tablespoons of the total water of the recipe and mix well to dissolve the yeast.
Cover the bowl with membrane and leave the mixture for 10 minutes until the surface is foamed, a sample that the yeast has been activated.
Keep 1 cup of the strong flour for the dough and add the rest along with the flour that inflates itself in a large bowl.
Add the salt and mix to go underneath.
Add the sugar, cinnamon, mastic and machete and mix.

Open a puddle in the center of the mixture where we first pour the activated yeast and then in doses the rest of the lukewarm water kneading until we have a soft dough collected in a ball. Whenever necessary, while working it, sprinkle it with some flour so that it does not stick in the hands.
Pour the dough into a floured counter and ferment for 7-8 minutes, until it is polished and soft and supple.
Transfer to a slightly oiled bowl, cover it and allow it to rise for about 40 minutes in a warm place.

tandoori-turkey.name: Tandoori Turkey
instructions: Toast spices in a skillet over medium heat, swirling often, until fragrant, about 2 minutes. Let cool. Working in batches, finely grind mixture in a spice mill. DO AHEAD: Can be made 1 month ahead. Store airtight at room temperature.

Toast spices in a skillet over medium heat, swirling often, until fragrant, about 2 minutes. Let cool. Working in batches, finely grind mixture in a spice mill. Sift through a medium-mesh strainer into a small bowl; stir to combine. DO AHEAD: Can be made 1 month ahead. Store airtight at room temperature.
Pat turkey dry with paper towels. Rub inside and out with salt; transfer to roasting bag. Stuff turkey with cardamom pods and cumin seeds, then onion, celery, and garlic.
Purée all ingredients in a blender. Pour marinade into roasting bag. Smear all over turkey. Tie bag and arrange turkey, breast side down, in a large heavy roasting pan. Refrigerate overnight.
Let turkey stand in a bag at room temperature for 1 hour. Turn breast side up. Create steam holes in bag if required by manufacturer's instructions.

Preheat oven to 400°. Roast turkey for 30 minutes. Reduce heat to 350°. Roast until an instant-read thermometer inserted into the thickest part of the thigh (puncturing bag with thermometer) registers 160°, about 1 1/2 hours longer. Cut top of bag open, being careful of steam and juices, and pull back over turkey. Roast turkey until breast is deeply browned but not burned and a thermometer inserted into thigh registers 165°, 15–30 minutes longer.
Transfer to a platter. Let rest for at least 20 minutes before carving.
Meanwhile, strain juices into a large saucepan. Spoon fat from surface. Simmer over medium heat until sauce is reduced to 3 1/2 cups, about 20 minutes.
Carve turkey. Serve with accompanying gravy.

tangerine-cake-economical-and-recipe-tel.name: Tangerine Cake - Economical And Recipe
tangerine-cake-economical-and-recipe-tel.original_name: Bolo de tangerina- Receita Económica e Fácil
instructions: Break the eggs into a bowl, add the sugar and oil and wrap. Reserve.
Wash the tangerines, eliminate the lumps, cut them into pieces and crush them with the magic wand.
Add the crushed tangerine to the previous preparation and then add the flour and yeast. Involve until it's fully embedded.
Garnish a form with butter and sprinkle flour. Lay the dough in shape and put it in the oven, preheated at 180°C, for about 30 minutes. Take it off, let it cool down and unshape it.

tangerine-jam-argiro.name: Tangerine Jam
instructions: We choose unbleached mandarins, medium in size, mature but tight and preferably without pits. (Otherwise remove the pits).
Wash the tangerines and remove the stems.
In a pot with boiling water, immerse the mandarins whole and boil for 4-5′.
Remove them with a drilled spoon and add them to a bowl with cold water to cut the boil.
We're dumping the water.
Then, with a sharp knife, cut the tangerines into thin slices with flesh and skin together.
With scissors, remove the thin white membranes in the compounds.
If there are pits, we gather them all and tie them to a tulip. They have a high pectin content and will help to tie the jam nicely.
After boiling, remove the tulpan with the pits, after fixing it very well.
In a clean pot with thick base, add the tangerines and 5 cup water.
Allow to boil for 15 minutes on medium to high heat, until the skin softens slightly.
Then add the 3 cup sugar.
Mix to melt the sugar.
From the time it boils, mix regularly with a wooden spoon.
Boil on medium to strong heat, stringing regularly for about 30′ until it darkens slightly and things.
In the final minutes of boiling, add the lemon juice and let it boil.
Equalize hot jam in hot sterile jars.
Fill the jars with tangerine jam to the top.
Turn them over to the kitchen counter and let them cool completely.
We keep them in a cool and shady place in the kitchen.
If we open a jar, keep it in the refrigerator.

tantuni-in-bazlama-lezzet.name: Tantuni In Bazlama
instructions: 1. Bake in jealous fire until meat is taken into a nonstick pan.
2. Add olive oil and butter and add onions and continue roasting.
3. When the onions lose their elbow, add spices and continue roasting in jealous fire.
4.   Take a large container for the pool.
5. Add salt and yogurt on the yeast and sugar.
6. Slowly pouring the water, knead the dough.
7.  Daha sonra hamuru 30 dakika dinlendirip mayaya gelmesini bekleyin.
8. Take the desired size of the chopped dough and round.
9. Cook the cloths on the basis and in the pan.
10. Finely chop onions for onion salad.
11. 11. Peel the shells of tomato and chop the cube cube.
12. The fine coast of Maydanosu.
12. August Wash and squeeze water for the pain of the onion.
14 July Add and mix all the ingredients into a coarse seasoning and oil.
15. To lubricate the basilicas, press it on the meat and put it into the salad and rub it.
16.  Afiyet olsun.

tarragon-pastry-lezzet.name: Tarragon Pastry
instructions: 1. Flour, powder sugar, salt, water and yeast and knead dough.
2. Leave to rest for 1 hour.
3. The inner mortar inside the necessary materials of the small chopp all shielded in a container.
4. Then cut the dough into the ratchet size and round
5. Share and close the edges.
6. Index to oven tray.
7. 180 degree of bake dough u cook until the car gets angry.
8. Serve hot.

tartlet-with-feta-cheese-tomatoes-and-he.name: Tartlet With Feta Cheese, Tomatoes And Herbs

ingredients.item: 4 tablespoons of chopped fresh mixed grass

instructions: 1. From the first to mind among plenty of ingredients.
2. Mix salt, butter, egg yolk and cold water in the kitchen robot.
3.  Unu eleyerek ekleyin.
4. The dough kneading by spreading flour on the work.
5. Wait for 30 minutes in the refrigerator in a nylon bag.
6. Oil tarp moulds.
7. Place the dough into moulds.
8. Cook in oven with 200 degrees after waiting in the refrigerator for 15 minutes.
9. Leave. from moulds to cool.
10. Mix soft cheese, eggs, creams and grass thoroughly in a bowl.
11. 11. Share the mixture into tartars.
12. Place one on the grounds in the form of hard whitepeyniri cube.
12. August Cherry tomatoes are divided into two.
14 July Cook for 15 minutes on the oven with 160 joints.
15. Serve by decorating Maydanozla.

tastys-purple-goddess.name: Tasty’s Purple Goddess

ingredients.prep: thinly sliced, plus more for garnish

instructions: Make the salad: Add the scallions, cucumbers, dill, cabbage, red onion, chives, and salt to a large bowl and toss to combine. Let sit for 5–10 minutes while you make the dressing.

In a blender, combine the beets, garlic, shallot, blackberries, basil, kale, nutritional yeast, lemon juice,  olive oil, cashews, 1 tablespoon balsamic vinegar, and 1 teaspoon salt. Blend until completely smooth, then add more vinegar and salt to taste. Add 1–2 tablespoons of water as needed to loosen the dressing to your desired consistency.
Pour about half of the dressing over the salad and toss until evenly coated. Add more dressing as needed.

Transfer the salad to a serving bowl and top with more chives and dill, if desired. Serve immediately with the remaining dressing and blue corn chips alongside. The salad will keep in the refrigerator for up to 2 days.
Enjoy!

tater-tots-with-gochujang-cheese-sauce.name: Tater Tots With Gochujang Cheese Sauce
instructions: Preheat an air fryer to 188°C (188 degrees C).
Cook potato nuggets in the preheated air fryer until crispy and heated through, about 15 minutes, shaking the air fryer basket halfway through cooking time.

Meanwhile, heat a saucepan over medium-high heat. Add Cheddar cheese and cream; whisk until cheese is melted, about 5 minutes. Add gochujang and salt and stir until combined. Drizzle sauce over potato nuggets.

tempura-battered-cauliflower-air-fryer-f.name: Tempura Battered Cauliflower: Air Fryer, Fried Or Baked
tempura-battered-cauliflower-air-fryer-f.original_name: Coliflor rebozada en tempura: en freidora de aire, frita o al horno
instructions: In a big saucer, water with some salt. Add cauliflower and cook them for 5 minutes to soften cauliflower slightly. Listen to her and let her get cold.
In a bowl, mix wheat flour, powder leaven, salt and black pepper. Add cold beer to the flour mixture and remove with a fork until a homogenous mass with no groups.

Warm up enough sunflower oil in a large frying pan with medium-high heat. Make sure the oil's hot enough before we start frying. Summerize every cauliflower floret into the bouncing dough, making sure you cover completely. Carefully put the cauliflower florettos bounced into hot and cold oil till they are golden and crispy across. You may have to fry them in several pits so they don't pile them up in the frying pan.
Once fried, remove cauliflower pellet with a frying and place them on absorbing paper to remove excess oil.

thai-beef-salad.name: Thai Beef Salad

ingredients.prep: about 1 inch/2.5 cm thick; see Tip
also known as Boston or Bibb, leaves torn into bite-size pieces

instructions: Preheat the broiler, with a rack positioned so that the meat will be 3 to 4 inches (7.5 to 10 cm) from the heat. Line the bottom of a broiler pan with foil for easier clean-up, if you like, then top with the broiler rack. Put the meat on the broiler rack, lightly coat with the oil, and season with salt and pepper. Broil the meat for 5 to 7 minutes per side for medium-rare (err on the side of caution with the time; you can always cook it more). Transfer to a cutting board, sprinkle with salt, and let rest for 5 to 10 minutes.
Meanwhile, in a large bowl, combine the lettuce, cucumbers, tomatoes, onions, mint, and cilantro.*

Cut the meat against the grain into very thin slices and add to the bowl. (If the meat is still hot, wait a few minutes until it is warm or it will wilt the lettuce and herbs.) Drizzle the salad with just enough Chile-Lime Sauce to lightly coat and gently toss together. Top with the peanuts, if you like.

Prepare to *, then cover and refrigerate the lettuce mixture and the unsliced meat separately for up to 1 day. The meat can be sliced and added to the salad cold or you can warm it in a 149°C (150°C) oven, if you prefer.

If the salad will be served within 1 hour or so, cover the lettuce mixture and meat separately and leave them on the counter. People can help themselves and then toss their individual portions with the dressing.

thai-papaya-salad-pequerecetas.name: Thai Papaya Salad

ingredients.item: 300 g de papaya verde rallada (aprox. 2 tazas)
6 tomates cherry cortados por la mitad
1 cucharada de pasta de tamarindo (opcional pero recomendable)
3-4 thin cuts of steamed Asian sausage type sai krok or naem (you can replace them with boiled frankfurt)
Col in leaves or glutinous rice to accompany (optional)

instructions: We scrape the papaya with a thick paddle or with a mandolin. We let her slip into a pallet if she drops a lot of fluids.

At a big mortar (or bowl if we have no), we slightly crushed garlic and chilies. We added sugar, fish sauce, lime juice and tamarind dough. We mix well. We put in the tomatoes, the green beans and the pawned papaya. We pounding him with softness, just so they mix flavors but without breaking up the papaya
We add peanuts and frying cuts, remove again and fix salt if necessary.
We serve at once with crispy cabbage or glutinous rice leaves if we are to make them more complete.

the-fluffiest-vegan-pancakes.name: The Fluffiest Vegan Pancakes
instructions: In a medium bowl, add the flour, sugar, baking powder, and salt, and stir to combine.
In a medium bowl or liquid measuring cup, add almond milk, apple cider vinegar, and vanilla, and stir to combine.
Pour the liquid mixture into the dry mixture and whisk until smooth.
Let batter rest for 5 minutes.
Pour about ½ cup (65 grams) of batter onto a nonstick pan or griddle over medium heat.
When the top begins to bubble, flip the pancake and cook until golden.
Serve warm with maple syrup.
Enjoy!

tiki-cantaloupe-coconut-cocktail.name: Tiki Cantaloupe-coconut Cocktail
instructions: Slice a thin piece of rind off both ends of cantaloupe; cut in half crosswise and remove seeds. Scoop out flesh; set aside cantaloupe halves.
Transfer half of flesh to a blender; reserve remaining flesh for another use. Add coconut milk, tequila, Midori, lime juice, agave nectar, and ½ cup ice to blender and blend until smooth.
Divide cocktail between reserved melon halves and add ice to fill. Garnish with mint sprigs and lime wheels and serve each with 2 straws.

tiramisu-cake.name: Tiramisu Cake
instructions: To make the cake: Beat cake mix, water, egg whites, and oil in a large bowl with an electric mixer on medium speed until well combined, about 2 minutes. Divide 2/3 of the batter between 2 prepared pans. Stir instant coffee into remaining batter; pour into the remaining prepared pan.

Bake in the preheated oven until a toothpick inserted into the center of cakes comes out clean, 23 to 28 minutes. Cool cakes in the pans for 10 minutes, then turn out onto a wire rack to cool completely.
To make the syrup: Combine brewed coffee and coffee liqueur in a measuring cup; set aside.

To make the filling: Beat mascarpone, confectioners' sugar, and coffee liqueur in a medium bowl with an electric mixer on low speed until smooth. Cover with plastic wrap and place in the refrigerator to keep cool.

To make the frosting: Beat cream, confectioners' sugar, and coffee liqueur in a medium bowl with an electric mixer on medium-high speed until stiff. Fold 1/2 cup frosting into chilled filling until combined. Store frosting and filling, covered, in the refrigerator to keep cool.

To assemble the cake: Place 1 plain cake layer on a serving plate. Using a thin skewer, poke holes in cake, about 1 inch apart. Pour 1/3 of the coffee syrup over cake, then cover with 1/2 of the filling. Top with coffee-flavored cake layer; poke holes in cake. Pour another 1/3 of the coffee syrup over cake and cover with remaining filling. Top with remaining cake layer; poke holes in cake. Pour remaining coffee syrup on top. Spread sides and top of cake with frosting.
To garnish the cake: Place cocoa powder in a sieve and lightly dust top of cake. Run a vegetable peeler down the edge of chocolate square to form curls. Scatter chocolate curls over cake.
Chill cake in the refrigerator for at least 30 minutes before serving.

tomato-and-cabbage-tabbouleh.name: Tomato And Cabbage Tabbouleh

ingredients.prep: cut into 1”-thick wedges, then very thinly sliced crosswise (about 4 cups)

ingredients.item: Aleppo pepper or  0.5 crushed red pepper flakes

instructions: Place bulgur in a large bowl and add 1½ cups boiling water. Let soak until softened and water is absorbed, 40–45 minutes.
Toss bulgur, cabbage, onion, tomatoes, mint, oil, lemon juice, and Aleppo pepper in a large bowl to combine; season with salt.
DO AHEAD: Tabbouleh (without oil and lemon juice) can be made 4 hours ahead. Toss with oil and lemon juice just before serving.

tortellini-in-aromatic-sauce-with-mozzar.name: Tortellini In Aromatic Sauce With Mozzarella
tortellini-in-aromatic-sauce-with-mozzar.original_name: Τορτελίνια σε αρωματική σάλτσα με μοτσαρέλα

ingredients.item: 250 gromotsarella cut into small cubes (or messenian talagani or halumi Cypriot -always a mastic cheese)
250 gromotsarella cut into small cubes (or messenian talagani or halumi Cypriot -always a mastic cheese)

instructions: Start the tortellini, sautéing in olive oil in a pot the garlic for 2 minutes.
Add the tomatoes, herbs, salt, pepper and sugar.
Simmer the sauce over low heat for 45 minutes, to bind.
Meanwhile, boil the tortellines and drain them.
Add them to the aromatic tomato sauce, rub pepper and sprinkle the parmesan.
We mix very well.
Remove from heat and add the mozzarella cubes.
Mix and serve on a deep platter.
If we want to garnish with finely chopped mint and basil.

tortellini-with-italian-sausage-fennel-a.name: Tortellini With Italian Sausage, Fennel, And Mushrooms

ingredients.prep: trimmed, halved through core, thinly sliced lengthwise (about 3 cups), fronds chopped

ingredients.item: Package sliced fresh crimini (baby bella) mushrooms
Package dried tortellini with pesto filling or fresh tortellini with 3-cheese filling
Finely grated parmesan cheese plus additional

instructions: Heat oil in large nonstick skillet over medium-high heat. Add sliced fennel bulb, sausage, and mushrooms; sauté until sausage is brown and cooked through and fennel is almost tender, 12 to 15 minutes. Add garlic and fennel seeds; stir 1 minute. Stir in cream, then 1 cup broth; boil until liquid is reduced and very slightly thickened, 2 to 3 minutes.
Meanwhile, cook tortellini in large pot of boiling salted water until just tender but still firm to bite, stirring occasionally. Drain tortellini; return to same pot.

Add sausage mixture to tortellini in pot. Toss over medium heat until blended. Add spinach; toss gently until spinach wilts. Stir in ½ cup cheese; add more broth by ¼ cupfuls to moisten if dry. Season with salt and pepper, sprinkle with chopped fennel fronds, and serve, passing additional cheese.

toshis-noodles-with-seared-sea-scallops.name: Toshi's Noodles With Seared Sea Scallops
instructions: Bring a large pot of lightly salted water to a boil. Cook spaghetti in the boiling water, stirring occasionally, until tender yet firm to the bite, about 6 minutes. Drain and set aside.
Meanwhile, combine broccoli with 2 to 3 tablespoons water in a microwave-safe bowl; cover tightly. Microwave on high until tender, 3 to 4 minutes. Drain any liquid; set aside.
Combine soy sauce, mayonnaise, chili crisp, gochujang, sugar, garlic powder, and ginger in a bowl.

Heat oil and butter in a wok over medium heat until butter is sizzling but before it turns brown. Add onion; cook for 1 minute. Add scallops; cook and stir until seared, about 3 minutes. Add steamed broccoli; cook 1 to 2 minutes. Pour soy sauce mixture over top, add spaghetti, and toss until combined and heated through, 2 to 3 minutes.
Garnish with green onion and serve.

traditional-hot-or-cold-zucchini-cream-p.name: Traditional Hot Or Cold Zucchini Cream
traditional-hot-or-cold-zucchini-cream-p.original_name: Crema de calabacín tradicional caliente o fría

ingredients.item: 1 pastilla de caldo de verdura o 600 ml de caldo de verduras
2 cucharadas de aceite de oliva virgen extra (30 ml)

instructions: We wash and cut into cuts the 4 pumpkins (we can leave the skin if we want more color and nutrients).
Pelamos y troceamos la patata y 1oo g cebolla aproximadamente.
In a pint, we heated up 2 tablespoons of olive oil and revamed onion and pumpkin for 5 minutes with medium-low heat. Then we add the potato and we give it some laps as well.
We add 600 ml of vegetable or water broth with 1 pail and cook 20 minutes till the vegetables are tender.
Retiramos un poco de caldo (para ajustar la textura) y trituramos hasta obtener una crema fina.
Incorporamos 100 ml de nata y una pizca de nuez moscada, mezclamos y rectificamos de sal.
We serve hot or cold with fresh parsley and picket if we like.

traditional-pork-carnitas-comedera.name: Traditional Pork Carnitas

ingredients.item: 1/2 kg de carne de costilla de cerdo (en trozos (o de lomo, o una mezcla de ambas).)
1 taza de manteca de cerdo o manteca vegetal

instructions: Para comenzar, vamos a preparar los vegetales que usaremos para esta receta. Primero, lavaremos muy bien el cilantro y picaremos las hojas finamente, hasta completar al menos media taza.
In the case of onion, we will skin and cut them into cubes as well. We will also peel our garlic teeth and mince them very finely.
Por el momento, reservaremos todos los vegetales que hemos dejado ya preparados.

Then we'll try and get a pot of copper. Traditionally, these containers are used to prepare the carnets because they distribute the heat evenly. If we don't have one, we'll look for a useful equivalent to distribute well the cooking heat.
Pondremos a calentar la olla, y agregaremos la manteca.
Agregaremos los trocitos de ajo y los dejaremos cocinarse en la manteca durante unos tres minutos.
Acto seguido, picaremos a la mitad las naranjas, las exprimiremos y agregaremos el jugo a la manteca.
We'll add the meat to the pot, and then 6 cups of water, or enough to cover the meat. We will also add a touch of salt to our taste, a touch of pepper and a teaspoon oregano coffee machine.
Mezclaremos y dejaremos que la carne se cocine, tapada, a fuego medio, durante una hora.
Pasado este tiempo, destapa la olla y aumenta el fuego, para que el caldo se evapore.

Even with far less fluids, tender meat should start to get golden in butter. The process should be monitored with care to prevent the pork from drying to the point of dehydration. It removes to keep butter and meat integrated and if necessary put some light down. Do not exceed 20 minutes during this final stage of cooking.

At the time of serving, with hot tortillas, onion cubes, lemons cut into quarters or octaves, minced cilantro and a good salsa. Thus, every person will be able to prepare his or her carne tacos at the time of his or her lunch.

trotter-pastry-lezzet.name: Trotter Pastry
instructions: 1. Take cleaned pashes into a large pot.
2. Cook until you go over and thoroughly soften.
3. Dismantle the water of the pips.
4. Separate meat from bones and chop small pieces in shape.
5. melt 100 gr butter in the pan and mulch.
6. Soda, butter and eggs to the dough kneading container and cook.
7. Prepare a dough with soft consistency.
8. Take the dough into two lumps.
9. Open the balls in the ready-made yufka review.
10. melt 50 gr butter and rub.
11. 11. Take the crushed butter on the dough.
12. Broadcast the crochet meats to form two rings on yufka.
12. August Place the other yufka on and on.
14 July Cut the dough in order to stay in the bowls with a pot cover or a round mold.
15. Take two round börek dough into oiled oven tray.
16. Pour the remaining butter on the ground.
17. September Pre-heated 180 degree bake 20-25 minutes.
18. Serve hot.

trout-chowder.name: Trout Chowder

ingredients.item: Salt and ground black pepper to taste

instructions: Heat 1 onion, trout, beer, and lemon in a heavy-bottomed pot to a boil; reduce heat to medium and cook until trout easily flakes with a fork, about 15 minutes. Transfer trout to a flat work surface; remove and discard skin. Discard onion, beer, and lemon.
Cook salt pork in a large skillet over medium-high heat, turning occasionally, until evenly browned, about 5 minutes. Reserve drippings. Drain salt pork on paper towels.

Melt butter in the same skillet. Add celery and remaining 1 onion; sauté until almost tender, about 5 minutes. Transfer to the pot. Stir in chicken stock, potatoes, salt, and black pepper; bring to a boil. Reduce heat to medium-low; simmer until potatoes are tender, about 20 minutes.
Add trout, salt pork, and heavy cream to the pot; cook until chowder is heated through, about 5 minutes. Garnish servings with parsley and chives.

tuckers.name: Tuckers

ingredients.item: Food-grade pure rose extract (not rose water); optional

instructions: Cook first 3 ingredients in a medium sauce- pan over medium-low heat, stirring occasionally, until mixture is hot, dry to the touch, and starts pulling away from sides of pan, about 10 minutes.
Scrape dough into a heatproof bowl. Stir in vanilla and rose extract, if using. Add 1 drop of food coloring; stir well. Press plastic wrap on top of dough. Chill for at least 5 hours or overnight.
Preheat oven to 300°. Line a baking sheet with parchment paper or a silicone baking mat and stack it on top of a second sheet (this keeps cookie bottoms from browning too quickly).
Roll 1 tablespoon of dough into a ball. With your fingers, pinch the ball to form a 3-sided pyramid that ends in a point. Dip into white sanding sugar.

Bake cookies until lightly golden on top and slightly firm to the touch, 25-30 minutes. Let cool on sheet on a wire rack. DO AHEAD: Tuckers can be made 3 days ahead. Store cookies airtight at room temperature.

tumblers-argiro.name: Tumblers
instructions: Add water and sugar to a small pot.
Mix until the boiling begins.
Once it boils, stop mixing.
Boil for 3–4 minutes.
Add the lemon at the end.
Let the syrup cool completely.

tuna-salad-with-roasted-veggies.name: Tuna Salad With Roasted Veggies
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375˚F (190˚C).
On a baking sheet lined with parchment paper, add the green beans, potatoes, and lemon.
Drizzle the vegetables with 2 teaspoons of olive oil and season with salt, pepper, paprika, rosemary, thyme, and garlic. Mix until evenly coated.
Add the bell pepper halves to the center of the baking sheet and drizzle with the remaining teaspoon of olive oil, ½ teaspoon of salt, and ½ teaspoon of pepper.
Roast for 20 minutes until vegetables are golden brown. Let cool.
Make the tuna salad: combine the tuna, celery, Greek yogurt, salt, pepper, and parsley in a medium bowl.
Add a large handful of greens to 2 bowls. Divide the roasted vegetables and bell pepper halves between the bowls. Add a hard-boiled egg, if desired. Scoop the tuna salad into the bell peppers.
Serve with a balsamic vinaigrette, if desired.
Enjoy!

turkish-doner-kebab.name: Turkish Doner Kebab
instructions: Preheat the oven to 177°C (175 degrees C).

Combine flour, oregano, salt, garlic powder, onion powder, Italian seasoning, black pepper, and cayenne pepper in a large bowl. Add ground lamb and knead until thoroughly mixed together, about 3 minutes.
Shape seasoned lamb mixture and place into a loaf pan; set on top of a baking sheet.
Bake in the preheated oven, turning halfway to ensure even browning, for about 1 hour and 20 minutes.
Wrap loaf in aluminum foil and let rest for about 10 minutes. Slice as thinly as possible to make doner kebab pieces.

turmeric-honey-paste.name: Turmeric Honey Paste
instructions: Mix honey, turmeric, coconut oil, and black pepper together in a resealable container using a butter knife until thoroughly combined. Cover and store in the refrigerator.

tuscan-sausages-in-tomato-teleculinaria.name: Tuscan Sausages In Tomato
instructions: Golpeie ou pique levemente a pele das salsichas. Lave os tomates, escorra-os e pique-os grosseiramente.

Lay the butter on a pot, light it on the fire, let it melt, add the garlic teeth and the chopped onions, and stir until they begin to shine. Then add the tomatoes, wine, blond leaf and season with salt and spicy. Add the sausages, cover them and let them cook for 20 minutes or until they are fine.
Rectify the seasonings and serve with potato puree and sprinkled with chopped parsley or coriander.

24th-street-spritz.name: 24th Street Spritz
instructions: Bring celery, sugar, celery seeds, and 2 cups water to a boil in a medium saucepan. Reduce heat and simmer until celery is soft and liquid is reduced by half, 30–35 minutes. Strain through a fine-mesh sieve into a small bowl, pressing on solids. Strain celery syrup again, if desired, through cheesecloth-lined sieve (to make liquid clearer).
DO AHEAD: Syrup can be made 2 weeks ahead. Cover and chill.
Stir gin, lemon juice, and 1 cup celery syrup in a large pitcher. Divide among glasses filled with ice and top off with seltzer. Garnish with celery stalks and lemon wedges.
